rationale: The two papers clearly overlap in online review/WOM context, and the cited paper offers a potentially relevant mechanism source (review-writing effort/credibility inferences), while the citing paper studies how incentives change review content via enjoyment. However, the provided evidence includes only a DOI-level match (no in-text citation context), and the focal constructs differ (mobile cue effects on readers vs. incentives on writers). Given this sparse evidence, it is not safe to drop but not strong enough to clearly keep.
